AK’s FPL GW26 Team Reveal & Transfer Plans | 3x Top 5K Finishes
The Captaincy Master
@AK_FPL1
returns with his FPL GW26 Team Reveal. With three Top-5k finishes to his name, AK breaks down his latest squad, key calls, and captaincy thinking that would define the Gameweek ahead.
Link to all our FPL GW26 blogs
including scout picks, players to target, buy, hold, sell analysis, differentials, captaincy metrics, best forwards, and more. Completely free to access as well!
FPL GW25 Review
A much better Gameweek in terms of scores and we have not had one of these in a long while. I got a very small green arrow that should really be considered a grey arrow, as I went from 609k to 591k.
The theme of my defenders conceding late goals continued as Rodon conceded an 87th minute goal to Nottingham Forest, while Leeds were already leading 3-0. Just how the cookie crumbles sometimes..
Bruno captain
worked out well, although Haaland was a point better off, but we really do not need to fuss about a point on captaincy. Thiago scored but could not manage any bonus points, Rogers came through nicely out of the game against Bournemouth with a 10 pointer (goal and 3 bonus points) and Gabriel and Rice managed clean sheet points and DC returns respectively.
The team does not look as bad as it did a few weeks ago, now especially when I can finally triple up on Arsenal again for their double in Gameweek 26..
Further Read:
Top FPL GW26 Differential Picks to Target Ahead of Deadline
FPL GW26 Team Reveal
Since it’s a Double Gameweek for the league leaders, the natural thing is to go in with the 3 of the best options into the DGW. Since I am on 2, I am looking to add an Arsenal player for obvious reasons. For me,
Jurrien Timber
is the one to go for. He has been very unlucky to not score in recent weeks and I am confident of starts in both games. Since the FA Cup tie against Wigan is between the 2 league games against Brentford and Wolves (both away), I can see Arteta benching most of his first team against Wigan to start everyone he prefers in the league.
The next question is, who goes out? Rodon, Thiaw and Tarkowski all 3 can go to be honest, but at the moment, I feel like Rodon is the one that will make way. Tarkowski has playable fixtures in GW26 (vs Bournemouth (H)) and in GW29 (vs Burnley (H)), he is surely keeping his place over the other 2. Thiaw probably has just 1 playable fixture in Gw28 (vs Everton (H)) and I may still chance it vs Spurs (A) this week. However, Rodon’s next 3 look really tough and I do not see myself playing him in those games at all. Naturally, he is the one on the chopping block, as thing stand.
Further Read:
FPL Gameweek 26 Transfer Tips: Two Players to Buy, Hold, & Sell
Triple Captain- GW26 or save for later?
I will now talk about the
Triple Captain chip
. I won’t be lying that I do not fancy playing my TC chip on a defender in a DGW, even though defenders are much better this season than ever and Gabriel is as much of an attacker as a defender. However, if I keep my TC chip for later, it will have to be a DGW. The other 2 DGWs this season are probably going to be
GW33 and GW36
. I am planning to play my
WC in GW32
and BB in the following week in GW33. So that leaves us with DGW36 possibly. There is a decent chance that City make it into the FA Semi finals and resultantly, have 2 DGWs, one in DGW33 and one in DGW36. However, my only issue is that there is a scenario where the league might already be done by then and City may look to focus on UCL and FA cup instead (if alive by then) and may not play their best side twice in 3-4 days. It’s a bit of a long shot, but as an FPL manager, sometimes you have to think long term to make short term decisions.
At this point of time, I am 50-50 on the TC Chip and will be updating the readers regarding it before the deadline..
Update – 10/02/2026 -16:28 GMT (1 hour before deadline)
The team is set and the
Chip is active!
Triple captain chip on Gabriel. Rodon to Timber being the move this week. Good luck all!

FPL GW26 Scout Picks
FPL GW26 Scout Picks – Goalkeeper
Raya
(5.9m) comes in between the sticks for
Arsenal’s double gameweek (DGW) against Brentford and Wolves
in FPL GW26. It’s a no-brainer, really; Arsenal have the best defense in the league, and Raya is nailed. Unlike most of the rest of Arsenal’s defense,
Raya is the only nailed defensive option
, making him a great target for the DGW in 26.
Arsenal have the best xGA, the best goals against, and the most clean sheets in the league
– they are undeniably the best defensive outfit this season.
It is genuinely an option to triple up on Arsenal defenders
for this GW, which tells you just how good their defense is. We’re going for Raya plus one other (no guesses for who our other Arsenal defender is) as we think Raya is the most nailed out of the options for these two fixtures. Raya is our keeper for FPL GW26.
Further Read:
FPL Gameweek 26 Transfer Tips: Two Players to Buy, Hold, & Sell
FPL GW26 Scout Picks – Defenders
Gabriel
(7.1m),
Chalobah
(5.8m) and
Munoz
(5.8m) make up our back three for FPL GW26…
Gabriel
is naturally our other Arsenal defensive asset for DGW26 as he is the
most nailed option after Raya
. One could suggest that Saliba is just as nailed as Gabriel for these two fixtures, but we’re sticking with the most expensive defender in FPL for his minutes security and goal threat.
Gabriel took home a 6-point haul in GW25
thanks to his side’s clean sheet. We, along with the rest of the FPL community and the 41.5% of managers that own him, will be hoping for at least double digits in this DGW!…
Gabriel is also the captain of our scout picks team for DGW26.
Chalobah
comes into the backline, replacing his teammate Sanchez for this round of PL fixtures. Sanchez drops out of the team principally because of the risk of rotation amongst the Arsenal backline (therefore rendering Raya the best option after Gabriel), and should still be considered a good option for this mid-week fixture against Leeds.
Why Chalobah?
Chalobah has the best defcon/90 and xdefcon amongst Chelsea defenders
(
FFH
), and therefore offers a security of points that other assets cannot.
His 1.03 defcon/90 is substantially better than the next best in the Chelsea squad
, though it must be said his goal threat is minimal. Leeds are a decent side, but with Chelsea chasing top four, you’d fancy them to win it. With high CS potential and great defcon numbers, Chalobah makes the team for FPL GW26.
Finally, we have Crystal Palace’s
Munoz
. Back from injury, Munoz helped his side to a 0-1 win over Brighton on Sunday, making it a
first CS in 3 games
for the Colombian right back.
8 clean sheets plus 6 goal contributions pushes Munoz into the top 3 for points per match amongst defenders
(behind only Gabriel and Disasi?) with 5.7 PPG. His injury forced many FPL managers, and indeed Glasner himself, into a rethink, but now he’s back, we all seem to be scratching our heads at how to get him back into our teams!…
With
Burnley at home in FPL GW26
, Munoz is an excellent option considering the CS potential and his attacking abilities. Since returning to the side, Palace have looked far more solid, and though his attacking numbers have not yet reached their pre-injury heights, the haul potential is certainly there. Munoz is a great option for GW26 and beyond due to his versatility of points scoring and his minutes security – BUY BUY BUY!

FPL GW26 Scout Picks – Midfielders
Wirtz
(8.4m),
Rice
(7.5m),

(7.6m),

F
(9.8m), and

(6.3m) make up our midfield five for FPL GW26…
Wirtz
comes into the team off the back of a blank against Man City in FPL GW25. He and his teammates face another tough test as they travel to the north east of England to face
Sunderland away
from home in FPL GW26, but we’re backing Liverpool’s record midfield signing in this one!
In the last 6 GWs, Wirtz has 4 goal contributions,
and in the last 4 GWs, he has 3. The German has been in fine form since Christmas,
creating 11 chances for his teammates across the month of January.
He has
4 double-digit hauls in the last 7 GWs
, and, having blanked against City, that surely means he’ll haul in GW26 (because that’s how probabilities work)… Sunderland are a decent side, but their
massive xGA overperformance
is slowly starting to catch up with them – I’m seeing shades of Nottingham Forest last season all over again! Wirtz is a great pick in FPL due to his great form, decent underlying stats, and fantastic fixtures over the next few GWs.
Rice
keeps his spot in the team for the double GW first and foremost due to his minutes security. The Englishman scored
5 points in FPL GW25
, taking him up into third spot for points scored by midfielders in FPL with
134
. Rice is a consistent FPL points scorer through defcon, CS, bonus, and goal contributions – a truly versatile FPL asset. With more exciting players such as Havertz, Gyokeres, Maduek,e and Trossard all at
risk of rotation
across this DGW, it makes sense to go for security over excitement, and for that reason we’re backing Rice once again!
Rogers
is the second midfielder to keep his spot in the AAFPL Scout Picks team, having picked up another goal in FPL GW25! The English midfielder now has
8 goals and 5 assists
in the Premier League so far this season, equaling his goal tally from last season with 13 games to go!
Brighton, Villa’s opponents for FPL GW26, rank 15th for xGA
in the league and have
conceded in every one of their last 5
Premier League matches. With Leeds and Wolves in GW27 and GW28, Rogers is surely a hold for now!
Bruno
retains both his spot in the team for the third GW in a row!
3 double-digit hauls in the last 4 matches
and attacking stats to die for, Bruno is a great option for the mid-week round of fixtures.
West Ham are looking somewhat resurgent at the moment, but so are United. The red devils dominated against 10-man Spurs in FPL GW25, with Bruno pulling the strings in midfield once again.
8 shots and 6 chances created
– we’re talking silly numbers at this point! With
Mbeumo seen limping after the match
, we’re going with just the one United asset for FPL GW26, though I wouldn’t blame managers

AK’s FPL GW27 Team Reveal & Transfer Plans | 3x Top 5K Finishes
The Captaincy Master
@AK_FPL1
returns with his FPL GW27 Team Reveal. With three Top-5k finishes to his name, AK breaks down his latest squad, key calls, and captaincy thinking that would define the Gameweek ahead.
Link to all our FPL GW27 blogs
including scout picks, players to target, buy, hold, sell analysis, differentials, captaincy metrics, best forwards, and more. Completely free to access as well!
FPL GW26 Review
I was expecting a much better Gameweek considering I had triple Arsenal and also a TC Chip activated on Gabriel. Having only managed 65, thanks to a Thiaw double-digit haul, I am on a small red arrow for the week, with my overall rank dropping from 591k to 596k.
DGW26 was a reminder of a very basic law that has been true for FPL since the era of Leighton Baines. “Never Captain a Defender”. Let alone playing the TC chip on one. And I let go of all those rules and played the TC on Gabriel.
Except for Thiaw’s 11-pointer and Rice getting 14 points across the DGW, there is not much to talk about as everyone else blanked or did not come close to a haul.
Now that the TC chip is out of the way, the plan is to dead-end the team into
Blank GW31
and (most likely) play the
Wildcard in GW32
. The preparations for this next phase has to start now.
FPL GW27 Team Reveal
The team looks fine this Gameweek. Arsenal triplets playing Spurs away from home does not sound as great, but looking at Spurs form and performances this season, it has not been a fixture to think about too much. Andersen is not ideal but still playable against Sunderland away, should at least get some DC points if not a Clean sheet. Thiaw is firmly sat on the bench against Manchester City away this week. Tarkowski, I also want to bench this Gameweek against a tough United side that knows how to score this season.
In the midfield, Enzo (Burnley H) and Rogers (Leeds H) play very favourable games this week, while Bruno is a solid pick in any given fixture. With 2.3 million in the bank, I am planning to move Potts out and upgrade him to a Brentford midfielder this week.
Due to the lack of funds, it has to be
Dango Ouattara
, as Schade is unattainable due to budget constraints.
Captaincy is yet to be decided for this Gameweek, but doubts around Haaland’s fitness may make the decision a bit easier to put the armband on Bruno, but we shall see..

FPL Gameweek 27 Transfer Tips: Two Players to Buy, Hold, & Sell
After the madness of DGW26, GW27 finally gives us something managers secretly fear: time to think.
No frantic 48-hour turnaround. No “just click something and pray.”
Just clarity — and the uncomfortable responsibility that comes with it. With the latest FA Cup results:-
The doubles are getting clearer.
The blanks are getting defined.
Fixture swings are now very real.
This is where seasons are won or quietly bled away — not through heroics, but through discipline.
So no fluff. No hedge words. Straight into
BUY–HOLD–SELL
, sponsored by …. Well, nobody. We are poor, but you can help
here
if you want
Link to all our FPL GW27 blogs
including scout picks, players to target, buy, hold, sell analysis, differentials, captaincy metrics, best forwards, and more. Completely free to access as well!
FPL GW27 Transfer Tips: Two Players to BUY
João Pedro (£7.7m) – FWD, Chelsea – 45.7% TSB
Chelsea aren’t just scoring goals right now — they’re suffocating opponents with volume.
Over the last 10 Gameweeks:
Data from
stats.allaboutfpl.com
(Fully Free)
Chelsea rank 1st for goals scored and xG
Burnley rank 20th for goals conceded and xGC
João Pedro ranks 1st among all players for goals, assists
and
total FPL points
Now combine that with the fixture: Chelsea at home to Burnley, projected to be the highest-scoring game of the Gameweek.
Burnley’s defensive collapse is especially severe in the box:
They concede a huge volume of big chances
Shots in the box dominate their concession profile
Wide-to-central transitions are their biggest weakness
Pedro thrives exactly there. He leads Chelsea for shot volume, attacks space aggressively, and arrives into finishing zones relentlessly. Add current form to that role, and this isn’t just a good pick — it’s one of the clearest buys of the week.
Verdict: BUY. Form, fixture, and role all screaming the same thing
Antoine Semenyo (£7.9m) – MID, Man City – 49.8% TSB
Manchester City don’t often get overlooked attacking options — but when they do, it’s usually because everyone’s staring at Haaland.
City host Newcastle this week and rank 2nd for projected goals. The context matters:
Newcastle play this match between two Champions League playoff legs
The second leg follows immediately after
Rotation, fatigue, and focus become unavoidable factors
Newcastle’s defensive weakness is also very specific:
They concede most chances through the centre and right side
Normally, that funnels value toward Haaland. This time, Semenyo may be the bigger beneficiary:
He operates heavily in those zones
He carries no fitness concerns
He’s started five straight matches
He’s the top-scoring midfielder in FPL
GW26 return:
1G, 1A
Upcoming fixtures:
NEW | LEE | NFO | WHU | Blank GW31
This is a classic “follow the zones, not the name” play.
Verdict: BUY. Same City upside, lower stress, massive momentum.
FPL GW27 Transfer Tips: Two Players to HOLD
Igor Thiago (£7.0m) – FWD, Brentford – 30.1% TSB
Igor Thiago is being sold for some strange reason.
Not because he’s stopped performing — but because managers are not staring at the long term horizon.
From GW27 to GW35, Brentford’s fixtures turn decisively green. That’s not a footnote — that’s the window where Thiago’s value compounds.
Selling now achieves three things:
You lock in fear-based decision-making
You lose team value unnecessarily
You almost guarantee a future buyback at a higher price
It’s about recognizing when a premium run is approaching — and not sabotaging yourself just before it begins.
Verdict: HOLD. Short-term discomfort, long-term payoff.
Erling Haaland (£14.9m) – FWD, Man City – 68.4% TSB
At this point in the season, selling Haaland is rarely tactical.
It’s emotional.
Three straight returns remind us of a simple truth: When City score,
he is still the centre of gravity
.
The upcoming run: NEW | LEE | NFO | WHU | Blank in GW31
Nothing hostile. Nothing extreme. Just steady opportunities — the kind Haaland historically turns into explosions without warning.
Managers talk themselves into selling him because:
The price feels heavy
The hauls aren’t constant
They want to feel proactive
But FPL doesn’t reward constant motion. It rewards patience with elite assets.
Verdict: HOLD. Boring decisions are often the right ones.
Haaland fitness update:
FPL GW27 Transfer Tips: Two Players to SELL
Bruno Guimarães (£7.0m) – MID, Newcastle – 11.3% TSB
This one is no longer about form or fixtures — it’s availability.
Bruno Guimarães has been ruled out for around eight weeks after suffering a serious muscle injury. He’s already flown to Brazil to continue treatment, confirming the severity.
The knock-on impact is huge:
He’s expected to miss multiple Premier League fixtures
He’s out for Champions League playoffs
He misses key domestic cup ties
His return timeline overlaps with international breaks and rotation-heavy periods
At his price point, you cannot afford a long-term absentee — especially during a phase of the season where fixtures and chips matter most.
Verdict: SELL. Dead money with no upside.
Marc Cucurella (£6.1m) – DEF, Chelsea – 16.9% TSB
Marc Cucurella’s situation is a textbook FPL exit.
He’s currently sidelined with a hamstring injury, and Chelsea have been deliberately vague about his return timeline. While early estimates suggest a 3–4 week absence, there is no firm date — and hamstrings rarely respect optimism.
What we do know:
He’s already missed games
He’s likely to miss upcoming clashes against Burnley, Arsenal and Aston Villa
There’s a strong chance his return comes after the March international break
For a £6.1m defender, uncertainty is fatal. Chelsea’s fixtures are tough, rotation risk will increase, and there’s no guarantee Cucurella returns straight into 90-minute security.
Verdict: SELL. Too expensive to wait, too unclear to trust.

Our Scout Picks team’s total of 51 was not enough to beat the Official Scout Picks score of 64 in FPL GW26. We remain
4 points

As always, our Scout Picks will be based on the eye test, stats, and analysis. But before we discuss player picks, let’s take a look at the fixtures for FPL GW27.

FPL GW27 Fixtures
Further Read:
FPL GW27 Transfer Targets & Watchlist Based on Stats & Eye Test
FPL GW27 Scout Picks
FPL GW27 Scout Picks – Goalkeeper
Sanchez
(4.9m) returns to the AAFPL Scout Picks team following a short absence in FPL GW26. Our Chelsea defensive pick of Chalobah proved an adequate replacement last week, hitting the Defcon threshold and delivering 3 points in spite of Chelsea’s late capitulation to Leeds. With
Burnley at home
, a fixture so good that FPL has had to add a darker shade of green to the game to show how easy it is, in FPL GW27, a Chelsea defensive asset is a must have!
FACT: Rosenier is unbeaten in the league with Chelsea and has picked up 13 points from a possible 15 since his appointment.
Chelsea’s ‘keeper is the
fifth highest scoring player in his position
and is just 7 points off breaking 100 points in FPL. Chelsea have tended to do well against teams towards the bottom of the table in recent games, having secured victories against both West Ham and Wolves in the last few weeks, so Blues fans will be hoping for a repeat in FPL GW27.
Burnley have the second worst xG and the fourth fewest goals
in the league. Their 2-3 victory over Palace may concern some, but their underlying stats in that game were poor (0.69xG and just 3 shots on target) – in their previous two PL matches they failed to score.
Burnley will be low on confidence following their exit from the FA cup at the hands of Mansfield town, whereas Chelsea will be looking to continue the fine form that new boss Rosenier has overseen.
Further Read:
Upcoming FPL Double/Blank Gameweeks | 25/26 FPL Season
FPL GW27 Scout Picks – Defenders
Gabriel
(7.1m),
Virgil
(5.9m) and
Kayode
(4.5m) form our back three for FPL GW27…
Gabriel
naturally keeps his spot in the team, despite his somewhat underwhelming performance in the DGW. Arsenal remain the premier defensive team in FPL, and all managers should be considering either a double or triple up on defensive assets from the Gunners. Gabriel remains the asset of choice for most managers (43.1% ownership), though
Timber and Raya
should also be considered.
Due to his
high ownership and potential rank threat
, Gabriel is our choice for FPL GW27. He ticks all of the necessary boxes in terms of
attacking output, underlying numbers, defensive solidity and defcon
. Though the fixture is not ideal, it is by no means a bad one, and one could feasibly see Gabriel picking up either a CS, an attacking return, or both when the
Gunners travel across town to the Tottenham Hotspur stadium
in FPL GW27.
Virgil
comes into the team for Liverpools favourable fixture against Nottingham Forest. The Liverpool captain scored
a massive 17 points in FPL GW26
, scoring a goal, hitting the defcon threshold, keeping a CS and taking home all three bonus points for the lucky few that own him. While it’s unlikely we’ll see another huge haul like that in GW27, Virgil remains a decent option due to his
versatility of point scoring
.
What’s more,
Nottingham Forest have scored just 2 goals in their last 3 games
, failing to score against Wolves who have one of the worst defenses in the league in FPL GW26.
Forest have actually scored the second fewest goals in the league this season
(25), though their expected data suggests they should have scored about 7 more goals than they actually have. It’s unclear how they will line up following the appointment of Pereira as their new coach, but this should still be targeted as a decent fixture from a Liverpool point of view.
Kayode
takes up the last spot in defense as Brentford host Brighton in FPL GW27.
Since January 1st Brentford have picked up the joint second most points in the league
and the Bees are now in touching distance of a Champions league place. Their form in the league is mostly attributed to Thiago’s heroics at the attacking end of the pitch, but their defensive stability should not be overlooked!
6 Clean Sheets and just 35 goals conceded makes them a mid-table side for defensive stats
, so why are we targeting their defenders for FPL GW27? Brighton, their opponents for this weekend, are in a spot of bother and are winless in 6 in the league.
The seagulls have lost both of their last 2 matches and registered less than 1xG on both of those occasions
. Hurzeler side lack identity and will surely struggle against such a well-drilled and well-coached side like Brentford. Kayode is nailed at RB, something that cannot be said for the rest of Brentford’s defenders, and offers decent attacking output and thus is our pick for GW27.
Brentford also have good fixtures beyond this week including a good fixture in Blank Gameweek 31.

FPL GW27 Scout Picks – Midfielders
We have selected a midfield four of
Wirtz
(8.4m),
Palmer
(10.6m),
Rogers
(7.6m) and
Bruno F
(9.8m) for FPL GW27…
Data from
stats.allaboutfpl.com
(Fully Free)
Palmer
comes into the team and takes the captains armband as Chelsea welcome Burnley to Stamford Bridge in this round of PL fixtures.
Palmer’s season-wide xGI of 8.63
is only marginally behind his actual goal involvements of 10, but it is his recent inspiring form which secures him a place in this team.
Five goal involvements in his last 2 matches
is stunning – is the old Palmer back?…
Against Leeds the Englishman managed
5 shots, 4 chances and 2 big chances created
. His xGI in that game was well over 2, and he could have certainly come away with more, missing a huge chance to win the game in extra time at the end (a chance worth 0.78xG!). More than that though,
Palmer has looked much more comfortable and much fitter in recent games
, owing perhaps to a slightly different role in the team, and a new training regiment from the new boss. Whatever the reason, Palmer looks back to his best, and with
a fixture against by far the worst defense in the league
(worst xGA and worst goals against) in GW27, Palmer is a fantastic pick.
Wirtz
keeps his spot in our team following another impressive showing against Sunderland in the league. The German
created 2 chances and took 5 shots
,
hitting the post in the 33rd minute
, but failed to register a goal or an assist in back-to-back games in the premier league for the first time since early December. Flo was in
